title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring incredibly crispy chicken coated in a vibrant, sweet, and spicy gochujang-based sauce. Its signature double-fry method ensures a satisfying crunch that perfectly complements the rich, sticky glaze. This recipe brings the authentic flavors of Korea's famous fried chicken right to your kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare and Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. In a large bowl, season the chicken with salt, black pepper, ginger powder, and garlic powder. Toss to coat evenly and let it marinate for at least 15 minutes at room temperature, or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a separate large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, and baking powder. In another small bowl, beat the egg and then add the cold water. Gradually pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients, stirring until just combined. A few lumps are fine; do not overmix. The batter should be thick enough to coat the chicken but still pourable.

instructions.title: Coat and First Fry the Chicken
instructions.text: Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated. Allow any excess batter to drip off. Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot or deep fryer to 350°F (175°C). Carefully add the chicken pieces in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove and place on a wire rack to drain.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is frying, prepare the sauce. In a medium saucepan, combine gochujang, ketchup, soy sauce, rice vinegar, brown sugar, honey or corn syrup, minced garlic, minced ginger, and sesame oil. Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, stirring constantly until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Extra Crispiness
instructions.text: Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 2-3 minutes, or until the chicken is deep golden brown and extra crispy. This double-frying technique is key for authentic Korean fried chicken texture. Remove and place on a wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat and Serve
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the hot, double-fried chicken to a large bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss gently but quickly until all pieces are evenly coated. Garnish with sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve hot and enjoy the irresistible sweet, spicy, and crispy goodness!

tips: For the crispiest chicken, ensure your oil temperature is accurate. Use a thermometer and double-fry as instructed.
Adjust the spice level of the Yangnyeom sauce by adding more gochujang for extra heat, or a pinch of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes). For less spice, reduce gochujang and increase ketchup.
Serve Yangnyeom Chicken immediately after coating to enjoy the best texture. It pairs wonderfully with pickled radish (mu-saengchae) to cut through the richness.
article:
Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken: Your Ultimate Guide to Sweet, Spicy, and Crispy Perfection

Korean fried chicken (KFC) has taken the world by storm, captivating foodies with its signature crispy exterior and succulent interior. But among the many delectable varieties, one stands out as a true fan favorite: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken. This iconic dish isn't just fried chicken; it's an experience – a harmonious blend of sweet, spicy, and tangy flavors coated over impossibly crunchy chicken pieces. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Korean fried chicken experience at home, you've come to the right place. Get ready to dive into the world of Yangnyeom Chicken and discover how to master this beloved Korean delicacy.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, often simply referred to as "Yangnyeom Chicken," is a popular style of Korean fried chicken coated in a thick, sticky, and vibrant sauce. The term "yangnyeom" (양념) itself refers to a seasoned sauce or marinade in Korean cuisine, and in this context, it describes the signature sweet, spicy, and savory glaze that makes this chicken so irresistible. Unlike American fried chicken, which often relies on a thick breading, Korean fried chicken is known for its ultra-thin, crackly crust, achieved through a unique double-frying method and the use of specific starches like potato or corn starch. The Yangnyeom sauce typically features a base of gochujang (Korean chili paste), gochugaru (Korean chili powder), soy sauce, garlic, ginger, sugar or corn syrup, and vinegar, creating a complex flavor profile that is addictive and deeply satisfying. It's the perfect balance of heat, sweetness, and a subtle tang that keeps you coming back for more.

## Ingredients You Need

To embark on your Yangnyeom Chicken journey, you'll need two main sets of ingredients: one for the crispy chicken and another for the irresistible sauce.

For the Chicken:
*   2-3 lbs chicken wings, drumsticks, or boneless thighs (cut into bite-sized pieces)
*   1 cup potato starch or corn starch (potato starch yields a crispier result)
*   1/2 cup all-purpose flour
*   1 teaspoon baking powder (helps with crispiness)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1/4 teaspoon black pepper
*   1 cup cold water (or sparkling water for extra crispiness)
*   Oil for deep frying (vegetable, canola, or peanut oil)

For the Yangnyeom Sauce:
*   1/4 cup gochujang (Korean chili paste)
*   2 tablespoons gochugaru (Korean chili powder, adjust to taste for spice)
*   1/4 cup soy sauce
*   1/4 cup corn syrup or honey (corn syrup gives a glossier finish)
*   2 tablespoons rice vinegar
*   1 tablespoon minced garlic
*   1 teaspoon minced ginger (fresh is best)
*   1 tablespoon sugar
*   1 tablespoon sesame oil
*   Optional: 1-2 tablespoons ketchup (adds a subtle tang and richness)

Garnish (Optional):
*   Toasted sesame seeds
*   Chopped green onions

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Mastering Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken at home is easier than you think with these detailed steps.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. This is crucial for crispiness. In a large bowl, combine potato starch, flour, baking powder, salt, and pepper. Gradually whisk in the cold water until you have a smooth, thin batter. Add the chicken pieces to the batter, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Let it sit for 10-15 minutes.
2.  **First Fry:** Heat about 3-4 inches of oil in a large pot or deep fryer to 325-330°F (160-165°C). Carefully add chicken pieces, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes, or until lightly golden and cooked through. Remove the chicken with a slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain. Let the chicken rest for at least 10 minutes. This resting period is vital for the double-fry technique.
3.  **Second Fry:** While the chicken rests, increase the oil temperature to 350-375°F (175-190°C). Return the rested chicken to the hot oil in batches. Fry for another 3-5 minutes, or until deep golden brown and exceptionally crispy. Remove and place on a clean wire rack to drain any excess oil.
4.  **Prepare the Yangnyeom Sauce:** While the chicken is frying, combine all Yangnyeom sauce ingredients (gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, corn syrup/honey, rice vinegar, minced garlic, minced ginger, sugar, sesame oil, and optional ketchup) in a small saucepan. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly, about 5-7 minutes. Be careful not to burn it.
5.  **Coat the Chicken:** In a large bowl or wok, pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce. Add the freshly double-fried chicken pieces and toss quickly and thoroughly until every piece is evenly coated in the glossy, sticky sauce.
6.  **Serve Immediately:** Transfer the coated Yangnyeom Chicken to a serving platter. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions if desired.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving restaurant-quality Yangnyeom Chicken at home is all about paying attention to a few key details:

*   **Double Frying is Non-Negotiable:** This is the secret to that signature shatteringly crisp Korean fried chicken crust. The first fry cooks the chicken through, and the rest period allows moisture to escape from the crust. The second, higher-temperature fry then creates the ultimate crunch.
*   **Use Potato Starch:** While corn starch works, potato starch often yields a lighter, crispier, and more delicate crust that is characteristic of authentic Korean fried chicken.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Fryer:** Frying too many pieces at once will drop the oil temperature, leading to greasy, soggy chicken. Fry in batches for optimal crispiness.
*   **Toss Right Before Serving:** For the best texture, only toss the chicken with the sauce immediately before serving. This prevents the sauce from making the crispy skin soggy.
*   **Pat Chicken Dry:** Excess moisture on the chicken will lead to a less crispy crust and potential oil splatters.
*   **High-Quality Gochujang:** The flavor of your sauce heavily relies on the quality of your gochujang. Invest in a good brand for the most authentic taste.

## Serving Suggestions

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a star on its own, but it pairs wonderfully with a few traditional accompaniments:

*   **Pickled Radish (Chicken Mu):** The crisp, tangy, and refreshing pickled radish is the perfect palate cleanser to balance the richness and spice of the chicken.
*   **Beer (Maekju):** The classic Korean pairing is "chimaek" (chicken and beer). A cold lager or light ale cuts through the richness beautifully.
*   **Rice:** A simple bowl of steamed white rice can help soak up extra sauce and round out the meal.
*   **Coleslaw or Salad:** A fresh, crisp salad or a creamy coleslaw provides a refreshing contrast.
*   **Soju or Soda:** For those who prefer a non-alcoholic option, a chilled soda is a great choice.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Yangnyeom Chicken recipe is fantastic, feel free to experiment to suit your preferences:

*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of gochugaru (Korean chili powder) to control the heat. For milder chicken, reduce or omit gochugaru. For extra spicy, add more or a dash of fresh chili flakes.
*   **Sweetener:** While corn syrup gives that classic glossy finish, honey or even brown sugar can be used as alternatives. Each will impart a slightly different flavor profile.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Use a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend and ensure your soy sauce is gluten-free (tamari).
*   **Chicken Cut:** While wings and drumsticks are traditional, boneless chicken thighs or even chicken breast can be used. Just adjust frying times accordingly (boneless pieces cook faster).
*   **Air Fryer Method:** For a slightly healthier take, you can air fry the breaded chicken (sprayed with oil) until crispy, then toss in the sauce. The texture won't be identical to deep-fried, but it's a good alternative.

## Frequently Asked Questions

*   **Can I make Yangnyeom Chicken ahead of time?**
    While the sauce can be made a day or two in advance and stored in the refrigerator, it's best to fry the chicken and toss it in the sauce just before serving to maintain maximum crispiness.
*   **What if I don't have a deep fryer?**
    A large, heavy-bottomed pot (like a Dutch oven) works perfectly for deep frying. Just be sure to use a thermometer to monitor the oil temperature accurately.
*   **Is Yangnyeom Chicken very spicy?**
    The spice level can vary. Traditional Yangnyeom Chicken has a noticeable kick from the gochujang and gochugaru, but it's usually balanced by sweetness and tang. You can easily adjust the amount of gochugaru to make it milder or spicier to your liking.
*   **Why is my chicken not crispy?**
    The most common reasons for non-crispy chicken are overcrowding the fryer, not double-frying, or not using a high enough oil temperature during the second fry. Ensure your chicken is patted very dry before coating, too.
*   **Can I bake the chicken instead of frying?**
    You can bake or air fry the chicken for a healthier alternative, but the signature crispiness achieved through double-frying will be difficult to replicate entirely. If baking, aim for a high temperature (around 400°F/200°C) until golden and cooked through.

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is more than just a meal; it's a culinary journey that promises an explosion of flavors and textures. With this comprehensive guide, you now have all the tools and knowledge to create your own perfectly crispy, sweet, spicy, and utterly addictive Yangnyeom Chicken at home. Gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to impress your taste buds and loved ones with this incredible Korean classic. Enjoy!
